tasks.notes:
Set the first layer of local router security.

Key Commands

  • hostname R1
  • enable secret <pw>
  • line console 0
  • password <pw>
  • login
  • service password-encryption

Secure remote access on the router and keep unused access limited.

Key Commands

  • line vty 0 4
  • password <pw>
  • login
  • exec-timeout 5 0
  • transport input telnet ssh

Finish basic hardening and verify the active access settings.

Key Commands

  • banner motd #Authorized access only#
  • show running-config
  • show ip interface brief
  • copy running-config startup-config

Check which side is DCE, set clocking only there, then bring the serial link up.

Key Commands

  • show controllers serial 0/0/0
  • interface s0/0/0
  • ip address <ip> <mask>
  • clock rate 64000
  • no shutdown

Create the VLANs, put access ports in the right VLAN, and verify membership.

Key Commands

  • vlan 10
  • name SALES
  • interface f0/1
  • switchport mode access
  • switchport access vlan 10
  • show vlan brief

Build the trunk correctly before expecting VLAN traffic to cross switches.

Key Commands

  • interface g0/1
  • switchport trunk encapsulation dot1q
  • switchport mode trunk
  • switchport trunk allowed vlan <list>
  • show interfaces trunk

Route between VLANs with router subinterfaces and 802.1Q tagging.

Key Commands

  • interface g0/0.10
  • encapsulation dot1Q 10
  • ip address <ip> <mask>
  • interface g0/0
  • no shutdown

Check the most common ROAS breakpoints first: VLAN, trunk, subinterface tag, and gateway.

Key Commands

  • show vlan brief
  • show interfaces trunk
  • show running-config interface g0/0.10
  • show ip interface brief

Verify the router subinterfaces and client gateways before chasing anything more advanced.

Key Commands

  • show running-config interface g0/0.10
  • show interfaces trunk
  • show ip route
  • ping <gateway-ip>

Use local user accounts instead of only line passwords.

Key Commands

  • username admin secret <pw>
  • line console 0
  • login local
  • line vty 0 4
  • login local

tasks.fileName: 011 - Message of the Day and Login Banners.pkt
tasks.notes:
Set clear warning banners for anyone reaching the device.

Key Commands

  • banner motd #Authorized users only#
  • banner login #Disconnect if you are not approved#

Name VLANs clearly so the switching table stays readable.

Key Commands

  • vlan 10
  • name SALES
  • vlan 20
  • name HR
  • show vlan brief

Use CDP to discover directly connected Cisco devices and ports.

Key Commands

  • show cdp neighbors
  • show cdp interface

Use detailed CDP output to learn IPs, platforms, and remote interface details.

Key Commands

  • show cdp neighbors detail
  • cdp run
  • no cdp enable

Know the initial setup dialog flow, then verify what it created from the CLI.

Key Commands

  • show running-config
  • show ip interface brief
  • copy running-config startup-config

Loopbacks stay up and are useful for router ID and stable reachability.

Key Commands

  • interface loopback0
  • ip address <ip> <mask>
  • show ip interface brief

Enable basic port security on an access port before tuning limits or actions.

Key Commands

  • interface f0/1
  • switchport mode access
  • switchport port-security
  • show port-security interface f0/1

Add MAC limits and sticky learning so the switch remembers the allowed host.

Key Commands

  • switchport port-security maximum 1
  • switchport port-security mac-address sticky
  • show port-security address

Tune violation behavior and verify whether the interface shuts down or only logs.

Key Commands

  • switchport port-security violation shutdown
  • switchport port-security violation restrict
  • show port-security interface f0/1

Use this as a quick checkpoint for the early CCNA build topics covered so far.

Key Commands

  • show running-config
  • show ip interface brief
  • show vlan brief
  • show interfaces trunk
  • ping

Troubleshoot from interfaces upward and verify VLANs, trunks, and gateways first.

Key Commands

  • show ip interface brief
  • show running-config
  • show vlan brief
  • show interfaces trunk
  • ping

Allow basic remote CLI access only if the line password and VTY settings are correct.

Key Commands

  • line vty 0 4
  • password <pw>
  • login
  • transport input telnet

SSH needs a local user, domain name, and RSA keys before VTY access works.

Key Commands

  • ip domain-name lab.local
  • username admin secret <pw>
  • crypto key generate rsa
  • line vty 0 4
  • login local
  • transport input ssh

Build static routes carefully and verify the next hop or exit interface is reachable.

Key Commands

  • ip route <network> <mask> <next-hop>
  • ip route 0.0.0.0 0.0.0.0 <next-hop>
  • show ip route
  • ping

Build static routes carefully and verify the next hop or exit interface is reachable.

Key Commands

  • ip route <network> <mask> <next-hop>
  • ip route 0.0.0.0 0.0.0.0 <next-hop>
  • show ip route
  • ping

Build static routes carefully and verify the next hop or exit interface is reachable.

Key Commands

  • ip route <network> <mask> <next-hop>
  • ip route 0.0.0.0 0.0.0.0 <next-hop>
  • show ip route
  • ping

Use RIPv2 with the correct networks and turn off auto-summary.

Key Commands

  • router rip
  • version 2
  • no auto-summary
  • network <major-network>
  • show ip protocols

Use RIPv2 with the correct networks and turn off auto-summary.

Key Commands

  • router rip
  • version 2
  • no auto-summary
  • network <major-network>
  • show ip protocols

Standard ACLs match only the source, so place them close to the destination.

Key Commands

  • access-list 10 permit 192.168.1.0 0.0.0.255
  • interface g0/0
  • ip access-group 10 out
  • show access-lists

Extended ACLs can match source, destination, and protocol, so place them near the source.

Key Commands

  • access-list 101 permit tcp 192.168.1.0 0.0.0.255 any eq 80
  • ip access-group 101 in
  • show access-lists

Named ACLs are easier to read and edit than numbered lists.

Key Commands

  • ip access-list extended WEB-FILTER
  • permit tcp any any eq 80
  • deny ip any any
  • interface g0/0
  • ip access-group WEB-FILTER in

Point the device at the correct NTP source and verify time sync.

Key Commands

  • ntp server <server-ip>
  • show clock
  • show ntp associations
  • show clock detail
  • clock timezone

Use NTP verification commands and make sure the clock source is trusted.

Key Commands

  • ntp server <server-ip>
  • ntp update-calendar
  • show ntp associations
  • show clock detail
  • ntp trusted-key
  • ntp auth
  • ntp auth key
  • 📷 Image
  • 📷 Image

LLDP is the vendor-neutral neighbor discovery option.

Key Commands

  • lldp run
  • show lldp neighbors
  • show lldp neighbors detail

Create the pool, exclude reserved addresses, and hand out the right gateway.

Key Commands

  • ip dhcp excluded-address <start> <end>
  • ip dhcp pool LAN
  • network <net> <mask>
  • default-router <gw>
  • show ip dhcp binding

Make sure hosts point to the correct DNS server and that the needed records exist.

Key Commands

  • ip name-server <dns-ip>
  • ping <hostname>
  • browser test from the client
  • default—gateway 192.168 .1.1
  • name—server 20.0.0.100

Use a fixed inside-to-outside mapping for a server that must always be reachable.

Key Commands

  • ip nat inside source static <inside-local> <inside-global>
  • ip nat inside
  • ip nat outside
  • show ip nat translations
  • R1(config)# interface g0/0
  • R1(config-if)# ip nat inside
  • R1(config-if)# exit
  • R1(config)# interface s0/3/0
  • R1(config-if)# ip nat outside
  • R1(config-if)# exit
  • R1(config)# ip nat inside source static 192.168.1.11 1.2.3.11
  • R1(config)# end

Dynamic NAT needs an ACL, a pool, and correct inside/outside interface roles.

Key Commands

  • ip nat pool PUBLIC <start> <end> netmask <mask>
  • access-list 1 permit <net> <wildcard>
  • ip nat inside source list 1 pool PUBLIC

PAT overload lets many inside hosts share one outside address.

Key Commands

  • access-list 1 permit <net> <wildcard>
  • ip nat inside source list 1 interface g0/0 overload
  • show ip nat translations

Send logs to the syslog server and timestamp them so events make sense later.

Key Commands

  • logging host <syslog-ip>
  • service timestamps log datetime msec
  • show logging
  • terminal monitor
  • logging synchronous

tasks.fileName: 041 - Password Recovery, Configuration Backup, IOS Upgrade.pkt
tasks.notes:
Focus on safe recovery, backup, and IOS file handling without losing the working config.

Key Commands

  • show version
  • show flash
  • copy running-config startup-config
  • copy startup-config tftp
  • boot system flash:<image>
  • confreg 0x2142
  • reset
  • config—register 0x2102

  • Sub say pahlay rommon mn ja kar password reset krna hai aur uskay bad password change krna hai then copy startup-config running-config krni hai takay nVram wali settings load ho jy usky bad new secret set krna hai aur phr run config ko start up config mn copy krna hai takay new secret set ho jy then backup bnana hai tftp server pay.
  • copy tftp flash
  • show flash
  • delete flash

Enable IPv6 routing, address the interfaces, and verify the route table.

Key Commands

  • ipv6 unicast-routing
  • ipv6 address 2001:db8:1::1/64
  • show ipv6 interface brief
  • show ipv6 route
  • https://youtu.be/3ba3BUZi36Y?si=EV0TTHwzAFurgD6T

Use this as a midpoint check for switching, routing, and service configuration accuracy.

Key Commands

  • show running-config
  • show ip interface brief
  • show ip route
  • show vlan brief
  • ping

Check interfaces, routes, and service settings before changing anything large.

Key Commands

  • show ip interface brief
  • show ip route
  • show vlan brief
  • show access-lists
  • ping

DTP negotiates trunks only when both sides support compatible switchport modes.

Key Commands

  • switchport mode dynamic desirable
  • switchport mode dynamic auto
  • switchport nonegotiate
  • show interfaces trunk

Set the VTP domain, mode, and password before expecting VLANs to propagate.

Key Commands

  • vtp domain <name>
  • vtp mode server
  • vtp password <pw>
  • show vtp status

VTP issues are usually domain, password, revision, or trunk problems.

Key Commands

  • show vtp status
  • show interfaces trunk
  • show vlan brief

Verify the root bridge and port roles before changing STP behavior.

Key Commands

  • show spanning-tree
  • spanning-tree vlan 1 priority 4096
  • SW1#sh spanning-tree sum 📷 Image
  • 📷 Image

Root bridge election is driven by priority first and MAC address second.

Key Commands

  • show spanning-tree
  • spanning-tree vlan 1 root primary
  • spanning-tree vlan 1 priority 4096

Bundle matching links into one logical port-channel and verify the protocol state.

Key Commands

  • interface range g0/1-2
  • channel-group 1 mode active
  • interface port-channel 1
  • show etherchannel summary

EtherChannel fails when member interfaces do not match on mode, speed, duplex, or trunking.

Key Commands

  • show etherchannel summary
  • show interfaces trunk
  • show running-config interface g0/1

Know when to use an SVI on a multilayer switch versus ROAS on a router.

Key Commands

  • interface vlan 10
  • ip address <ip> <mask>
  • ip routing
  • show ip interface brief

Check VLANs, trunks, SVIs or router subinterfaces, and the client default gateway in that order.

Key Commands

  • show vlan brief
  • show interfaces trunk
  • show ip interface brief
  • show ip route

Start with a clean single-area OSPF build and verify adjacency and routes.

Key Commands

  • router ospf 1
  • network <net> <wildcard> area 0
  • show ip ospf neighbor
  • show ip route ospf

Place interfaces in the right OSPF areas and verify the ABR forms neighbors correctly.

Key Commands

  • router ospf 1
  • network <net> <wildcard> area <id>
  • show ip ospf interface brief
  • show ip route ospf

Most OSPF issues come from area mismatch, wrong network statements, or passive interfaces.

Key Commands

  • show ip ospf neighbor
  • show ip ospf interface brief
  • show running-config | section ospf

OSPFv3 runs on IPv6-enabled interfaces after the process is created and enabled.

Key Commands

  • ipv6 unicast-routing
  • ipv6 router ospf 1
  • router-id 1.1.1.1
  • interface g0/0
  • ipv6 ospf 1 area 0

Use the same EIGRP AS on all routers and advertise the right networks.

Key Commands

  • router eigrp 1
  • network <net>
  • no auto-summary
  • show ip eigrp neighbors
  • show ip route eigrp
  • interface level summary in eigrp allow insated of area or ospf conf ig mode   📷 Image

Check the AS number, network statements, and passive interfaces first.

Key Commands

  • show ip eigrp neighbors
  • show ip protocols
  • show running-config | section eigrp

EIGRP for IPv6 needs the process up and enabled per interface.

Key Commands

  • ipv6 unicast-routing
  • ipv6 router eigrp 1
  • no shutdown
  • interface g0/0
  • ipv6 eigrp 1

PPP authentication only works when usernames, passwords, and hostnames line up correctly.

Key Commands

  • encapsulation ppp
  • ppp authentication pap chap
  • username <peer> password <pw>
  • show interfaces serial 0/0/0

PPP issues usually come from encapsulation mismatch, bad usernames, or clocking problems.

Key Commands

  • show interfaces serial 0/0/0
  • show running-config interface s0/0/0
  • debug ppp negotiation

Bundle multiple serial links into one logical multilink interface.

Key Commands

  • interface multilink 1
  • ppp multilink
  • interface s0/0/0
  • ppp multilink group 1
  • show ppp multilink

PPPoE ties a virtual template to the access interface and authenticates the client session.

Key Commands

  • bba-group pppoe global
  • virtual-template 1
  • pppoe enable group global
  • show pppoe session

Build the GRE tunnel first, then route the remote networks through it.

Key Commands

  • interface tunnel0
  • ip address <ip> <mask>
  • tunnel source <src>
  • tunnel destination <dst>
  • show interface tunnel0

Check basic IP reachability under the tunnel before troubleshooting GRE itself.

Key Commands

  • show interface tunnel0
  • show ip route
  • ping <tunnel-destination>
  • show running-config interface tunnel0

Bring up the BGP peering first, then advertise the expected networks.

Key Commands

  • router bgp <asn>
  • neighbor <peer-ip> remote-as <peer-as>
  • network <prefix> mask <mask>
  • show ip bgp summary
  • Jub Bhe bgp conf karna hai to tmam network ko advertise krna hai  📷 Image

BGP neighbors stay down when reachability, remote-as, or update-source is wrong.

Key Commands

  • show ip bgp summary
  • show ip route
  • show running-config | section bgp

Use HSRP for gateway redundancy and make the preferred router obvious.

Key Commands

  • standby 1 ip <virtual-ip>
  • standby 1 priority 110
  • standby 1 preempt
  • show standby brief

Verify the HSRP group, virtual IP, priorities, and interface status on both routers.

Key Commands

  • show standby brief
  • show running-config interface g0/0

Keep IPv4 and IPv6 ACL logic separate and apply each in the right direction.

Key Commands

  • access-list 101 permit ip <src> <wildcard> any
  • ipv6 access-list V6-FILTER
  • permit tcp any any eq 80
  • ipv6 traffic-filter V6-FILTER in

ACL problems are usually the wrong interface, wrong direction, or the implicit deny at the end.

Key Commands

  • show access-lists
  • show ip interface
  • show running-config interface <int>

Mirror the right source traffic to the analyzer port and verify the session state.

Key Commands

  • monitor session 1 source interface f0/1
  • monitor session 1 destination interface f0/24
  • show monitor session 1

Track reachability automatically and tie it to failover or route decisions.

Key Commands

  • ip sla 1
  • icmp-echo <target-ip>
  • frequency 5
  • ip sla schedule 1 life forever start-time now
  • track 1 ip sla 1 reachability

Treat this like a final build review and verify every major service before saving.

Key Commands

  • show running-config
  • show ip route
  • show standby brief
  • show access-lists
  • ping
  • copy running-config startup-config

Use end-to-end verification and isolate the failure domain before editing configs.

Key Commands

  • show ip interface brief
  • show ip route
  • show standby brief
  • show access-lists
  • traceroute
  • ping
